treatment_information.specific_treatment: thoracic endovascular aortic repair (TEVAR) and left subclavian artery coiling
treatment_information.treatment_response: The fistula was successfully treated, and the patient was hemodynamically stabilized.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient transferred for low blood pressure and bloody gastric tube drainage
Upper GI endoscopy revealed a mass-like lesion with an ulcer and red pigmentation in the upper esophagus
Biopsy performed for the esophageal lesion
Patient revisited due to hypotension and decreased thrill on the arteriovenous fistula
Upper GI endoscopy showed actively bleeding and pulsating vessel in the previous esophageal ulcer base
CT revealed right-side aortic arch with ALSA and suspected bleeding from the diverticular part of ALSA to esophagus
Thoracic arteriography confirmed extravasation from an aneurysmal segment of ALSA
Patient received TEVAR and left subclavian artery coiling
